summary: Justin explains the equity stack in multifamily deals, covering investor types (friends/family, accredited/non-accredited retail, institutional private equity, family offices, high net worth individuals), typical capital stack structures (70% debt/30% equity), and how to split equity between general partners (Class B) and limited partners (Class A shares) with example profit distributions.
takeaways: Friends and family typically invest $25K-$100K per person in early deals
Institutional private equity minimum investments typically $5M-$10M
Standard debt-to-equity split is 70% loan to 30% equity
In a $7M loan / $3M equity deal, 90% of equity ($2.7M) comes from Class A limited partners, 10% ($300K) from Class B general partners
Typical profit/cash flow split is 70% to limited partners, 30% to general partners when LPs bring 90% of equity capital
Family offices and ultra-high-net-worth individuals require strong track record and resume; they conduct extensive underwriting and can write checks of $1M-$2M+ quickly once relationship is built
Capital stack structures vary by deal type; combo approach recommended early—mix of retail syndication plus institutional private equity

skill_candidates.change: Learn to properly structure equity splits and capital stacks with Class A/B shares and profit distributions
skill_candidates.evidence: Justin walks through specific example: 70% loan, 30% equity, with 90/10 split between LP (Class A) and GP (Class B) capital, resulting in 70/30 cash flow distribution

title: Lesson 3: The Equity
